sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è risk-off a causa della guerra in corso tra Israele e Iran, con il Brent che raggiunge $109.05 (+7.80%) e il DXY che sale a 100.03 (+0.38%). Il VIX è in calo a 23.87 (-2.73%), ma rimane sopra 20, indicando un mercato difensivo. L'S&P 500 ha chiuso in leggero aumento (+0.11%) a 6582.69.
sentiment.reason_en: Market sentiment is risk-off due to the ongoing war between Israel and Iran, with Brent reaching $109.05 (+7.80%) and DXY rising to 100.03 (+0.38%). VIX is down to 23.87 (-2.73%), but remains above 20, indicating a defensive market. S&P 500 closed slightly up (+0.11%) at 6582.69.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato azionario ha chiuso in leggero aumento, ma la guerra in corso tra Israele e Iran ha aumentato l'incertezza. Il prezzo del petrolio è salito notevolmente, raggiungendo $109.05. Il dollaro si è rafforzato rispetto all'euro, con EUR/USD in calo a 1.15 (-0.17%).
market_impact_summary.en: The stock market closed slightly up, but the ongoing war between Israel and Iran has increased uncertainty. Oil prices surged, reaching $109.05. The dollar strengthened against the euro, with EUR/USD down to 1.15 (-0.17%).

audio_script_it:
Buongiorno e benvenuti all'aggiornamento finanziario di oggi, il vostro Morning Briefing quotidiano.

Mentre i mercati asiatici hanno chiuso la settimana con il Nikkei in calo dell'uno virgola due percento a cinquantatremila punti e lo Shanghai in flessione dell'uno percento a tremilaottocento punti, l'attenzione si sposta ora sugli sviluppi macroeconomici e sui mercati occidentali che riprenderanno la prossima settimana.

La chiusura dei mercati asiatici consegna un quadro misto, tuttavia, il sentiment attuale risulta essere prevalentemente risk-off. L'S&P 500 ha chiuso con un modesto aumento dello zero virgola undici percento a seimilacinquecentottantadue virgola sessantanove punti, mentre il Dollar Index (DXY) ha guadagnato lo zero virgola trentotto percento a centopuntiotrentuno. Il VIX, indice di volatilità, ha registrato un calo del due virgola settantatré percento a ventitré virgola ottantasette punti. Sui mercati obbligazionari, il TLT Bond USA a vent'anni ha avanzato dello zero virgola sessantuno percento a ottantasei virgola settantanove, con il tasso decennale Usa sceso al quattro virgola trentuno percento.

Sul fronte geopolitico, continuano le tensioni con il prezzo del petrolio che sale bruscamente a centonove virgola zero cinque dollari al barile, spinto dalle preoccupazioni legate al possibile conflitto in Medio Oriente. L'oro risulta in calo dell'uno virgola sessantotto percento a quattromila settecentodue virgola settanta dollari l'oncia.

In ambito macroeconomico, gli ultimi dati disponibili mostrano un quadro contrastante. Il mercato continua ad attendere nuovi dati, soprattutto quelli relativi al PIL Usa, in uscita la prossima settimana. La BCE ha recentemente mantenuto invariati i tassi, con il tasso dei depositi al due percento.

Mentre le borse mondiali osservano la consueta pausa festiva, i riflettori restano accesi sul comparto digitale che opera twenty-four-seven. Bitcoin ha registrato una lieve flessione dello zero virgola settantuno percento a sessantaseimilaottocentoundici dollari. Tra le altre criptovalute, Ethereum in calo dello zero virgola settantasei percento a duemila trentotto dollari, Solana scesa dello zero virgola sessantotto percento a settantanove virgola settantatré dollari e Binance Coin con una variazione negativa dello zero virgola ventuno percento a cinquecentonovanta virgola venticinque dollari.

Alla ripresa degli scambi, l'attenzione degli investitori sarà catalizzata dai dati macroeconomici in uscita la prossima settimana, in particolare quelli relativi al PIL Usa e altri indicatori economici. Nel frattempo, il sentiment risk-off potrebbe continuare a influenzare le scelte di investimento, sia nei mercati tradizionali che nel comparto digitale.

Spostiamo l'attenzione sul mercato delle criptovalute, dove abbiamo visto alcune mosse interessanti nelle ultime ore.

Bitcoin è sceso leggermente, con un prezzo attuale di $66,810, in calo dello 0.18%. Gli ETF su Bitcoin hanno registrato un afflusso giornaliero di $9.0M. Questo dato positivo potrebbe indicare una certa fiducia degli investitori nel mercato.

Ethereum è sceso dello 0.76% a $2,038, mentre Solana è in calo dello 0.68% a $79.73. Binance Coin (BNB) ha perso lo 0.21% arrivando a $590.25. Queste mosse sono in linea con il trend laterale del mercato.

L'indice Fear & Greed è a 12, in territorio di Estrema Paura. Questo livello suggerisce che gli investitori sono piuttosto cauti e incerti sul futuro del mercato. La correlazione con i dati macroeconomici recenti potrebbe essere un fattore importante in questo scenario.

In generale, il mercato delle criptovalute sembra essere in attesa di una direzione più chiara. Gli investitori stanno monitorando da vicino sia i dati macroeconomici che le notizie specifiche del settore crypto.

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.

The global mood is cautious, with investors monitoring economic data and geopolitical events. The US Core CPI Year over Year came in at 2.7%, and Non-Farm Payrolls increased by 178,000. The Dollar Index rose to 100.03, while the VIX index decreased to 23.87.

Traditional markets are closed for the weekend. On the last close, equities were mixed: the S&P 500 ended at 6582.69, up 0.11%, while the STOXX 600 was down 0.20% to 59.24. In the bond market, the US 10-year yield was at 4.31%, down 0.14%. Commodities saw significant movements, with Brent crude rising to $109.05, up 7.80%, and Gold decreasing to $4702.70 per ounce, down 1.68%.

Geopolitical tensions continue to influence market sentiment. The ongoing situation in the Middle East and US-China trade relations are being closely watched. The European Central Bank recently adjusted its deposit rate to 2.00%. The global M2 liquidity stands at $22.7 trillion, expanding with a US Year over Year increase of 4.9%.

While traditional markets observe their holiday break, the spotlight remains on digital assets, which are open 24/7. Let's turn to the current state of cryptocurrencies.

Moving on to the digital assets space...

Let's dive deeper into Bitcoin's current technical analysis. The recent BTC ETF daily net inflow of $9.0M suggests a positive sentiment among investors. However, the price action is stagnant, with BTC trading at $66,810, down 0.18% today. From a technical standpoint, the flows indicate a potential for a breakout, but the direction remains uncertain.

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and take a look at some of the major altcoins. Ethereum, Solana, and Binance Coin are all experiencing slight losses, with ETH at $2,038, down 0.76%, SOL at $79.73, down 0.68%, and BNB at $590.25, down 0.21%. Despite the current price action, these altcoins continue to show strong fundamentals and potential for growth.

In terms of market sentiment, the Fear & Greed Index is currently at 12, indicating extreme fear. This could be a sign that investors are overly cautious, potentially leading to a heavier breakout in the near future. As we continue to monitor the market, it's essential to keep an eye on the correlation between cryptocurrency and traditional markets, particularly with the upcoming macroeconomic data releases.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A wrap from Prais Alért. Al prossimo aggiornamento.

articles.title: The realities of economic security
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics presents a high-level analytical discussion of the realities of economic security, examining real-world trade-offs and vulnerabilities in today's geopolitically fractured world. Anjali Bhatt, host of PIIE Insider Live, moderates a candid discussion with PIIE experts: Warwick McKibbin, who presents global macroeconomic scenarios of the impact of the Middle East conflict; Cullen Hendrix, who discusses vulnerabilities of the global economy to the disrupted supply of commodities including food, fuel, and industrial inputs; and Jacob Funk…

articles.title: The future economic architecture of the eurozone
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics is pleased to host a high-level panel discussion on the future economic architecture of the eurozone, featuring Spanish first vice president and finance minister Carlos Cuerpo Caballero and ECB Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel, alongside leading experts Olivier Blanchard of PIIE and Ángel Ubide of Citadel. Following opening remarks by Cuerpo Caballero, the conversation explores how much monetary policy transmission and financial stability in the eurozone depend on deeper integration, the opportunities and challenges posed by rising long…

articles.title: Central banking for open economies in a changed world
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics holds a high-level discussion with central bank governors addressing some of the most pressing issues in open-economy monetary policy. The conversation focuses on the implications of profound changes in the international financial and geopolitical environment. The panel, moderated by Patrick Honohan, PIIE nonresident senior fellow and former governor of the Bank of Ireland, includes central bank governors Anna Breman (Reserve Bank of New Zealand), José Luis Escrivá (Bank of Spain), Chang Yong Rhee (Bank of Korea), and Martin Schlegel (Swiss…

articles.title: US-China trade update: Imports, exports, fentanyl flows, and beyond
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics hosts a timely virtual briefing on US-China trade and tech policy, just ahead of the anticipated high-level summit between Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping. With extensive public focus on tariffs, this session provides a clear, evidence-based update on the broader state of US-China economic and technology relations, drawing on the latest data and analysis from PIIE experts. Chad P. Bown presents fresh 2025 trade flow insights from his upcoming and recently published blogs on US imports from and exports to China, Marcus Noland offers…

articles.title: Decentering the dollar: A conversation
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics is pleased to host "Decentering the dollar: A conversation." As the Trump administration pursues major changes to long-standing trade and geopolitical arrangements, this session will examine the sustainability of the dollar's dominance, the prospects for coordinated international responses, and the implications for currencies, government debt, and global financial stability. Klaas Knot (PIIE; former governor, De Nederlandsche Bank; former chair, Financial Stability Board) will open with his perspective on global monetary dynamics,…

articles.title: What Beijing Is Learning From Operation Epic Fury
articles.snippet: Five weeks into the US-Israel air campaign against Iran, the world is tallying the economic wreckage: Brent crude at $114 a barrel, the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed to commercial traffic, and the International Energy Agency calling it the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market. Beijing is watching all of it. And not just the energy markets. Operation Epic Fury, launched Feb. 28, has given China’s military planners an unprecedented real-time window into how the United States wages high-end warfare, according…

articles.title_en: What Beijing Is Learning From Operation Epic Fury
articles.summary_it: L'articolo discute l'impatto della guerra tra Israele e Iran sui mercati energetici e come Pechino stia rispondendo a queste dinamiche.

articles.title: How the Iran War Became NATO’s Biggest Crisis
articles.snippet: President Donald Trump told Reuters on Wednesday that he is considering withdrawing the United States from the 76-year-old alliance after European allies declined to send warships to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to global shipping. "Wouldn't you if you were me?" he asked. Earlier this week, in an interview with Britain's The Telegraph, he called NATO a "paper tiger" and said Russian President Vladimir Putin shared the assessment. The spat has a straightforward trigger. The Strait of Hormuz, the narrow chokepoint between Iran and Oman through which…

articles.title: The Two-Week Window That Could Break Global Commodity Markets
articles.snippet: The familiar assumption used by markets remains in place, at least according to financial analysts: what has been priced is what matters. Oil is still elevated but not yet showing a disorderly pattern. LNG is tightening but still trading within a recognizable or conventional range. Freight rates are rising, insurers are repricing risk, and policymakers continue to signal control. On the surface, all these signs are showing a stressed but functioning system. The coming weeks will reveal which systemic risks-such as chain desynchronization or supply…

articles.title: U.S. Auto Industry Proposes Vehicle Fee to Replace Gas Tax
articles.snippet: The growing share of electric vehicles and the expected increase in EV sales this year amid soaring gasoline prices are reducing the revenues for the U.S. Highway Trust Fund, which pays for America’s roads. Most of the revenue for the fund comes from the 18.4% per gallon federal gas tax, which hasn’t been changed since 1993. Yet, over the past 30 years, the funding for the trust fund has been declining, due to inflation and the fact that EVs now represent 2.5% of total light-duty vehicles in operation in America, and the market…
